task_completion.description: OpenOffice Writer is launched and the document is being edited.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Apache OpenOffice Writer application window is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: An auto-generated Table of Contents is present.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Table of Contents block (typically with a gray background or dotted leader lines) is visible at the beginning of the document.

task_completion.description: Heading 1 and Heading 2 styles are applied to sections.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Main section titles (e.g., Project Description, Ecological Resources) and subsections are formatted with distinct, larger, bold heading styles.

task_completion.description: Page numbers are inserted in the document footer.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The bottom margin (footer) of the document pages contains visible page numbers.

task_completion.description: Formatted tables for Wildlife, Noise, and Mitigation are included.
task_completion.visual_evidence: At least three distinct grid-formatted tables are visible in the document, containing data like species names (e.g., Indiana Bat), noise levels (dBA), or mitigation strategies.

task_completion.description: The JSON data is synthesized into a substantial professional narrative.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Multiple pages of dense paragraph text (at least 25 paragraphs) are visible, rather than raw JSON data or bulleted lists.

integrity.description: The agent used the OpenOffice Writer GUI to create and format the document.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the OpenOffice Writer interface being actively used (menus, toolbars, typing), rather than just a terminal window running a script to generate the file.

integrity.description: The agent synthesized the data rather than dumping raw JSON.
integrity.visual_evidence: The document contains natural language paragraphs, not JSON syntax (like curly braces, brackets, and quoted keys).

privileged_info_for_vlm: The document must be an EIA Executive Summary for the 'Ridgeline Wind Energy Project' in Garrett County, Maryland. Look for keywords: 'Indiana Bat', 'Myotis sodalis', and 'dBA'. The document requires at least 7 H1 headings, 8 H2 headings, 3 tables (Wildlife Species Inventory, Noise Receptor Data, Mitigation Measures), and a minimum of 25 paragraphs of synthesized narrative text. Page numbers must be in the footer, and a Table of Contents must be present.
